mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals expansion in labor demand, which raises household income and consumption expectations, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment growth historically leads equity gains during recovery phases.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative more than 55% of the time over any 2-year period, hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in housing starts (HOUST) above 800 thousand units annualized signals a robust housing recovery, which stimulates construction employment and related consumer spending, positively impacting equities with a lag of about one quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts above 800k precursor to strong GDP growth in historical data.
failure_condition: If HOUST crosses above 800k but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in at least 40% of such events, or if the average forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate that both demand and supply sides of the economy are expanding in a synchronized manner, providing a stronger and more credible recovery signal that boosts equity valuations over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and INDPRO show positive month-over-month growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Synchronized expansion in durables and nondurables historically coincides with equity bull runs.
regime_hint: early recovery phase with broad-based expansion
failure_condition: If the joint condition produces positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns less than 60% of the time over a 5-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 2.5%), strong payroll gains (PAYEMS) are less likely to trigger immediate monetary tightening, allowing the expansion to persist and boosting equity prices over the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY is below 2.5% and PAYEMS shows positive monthly change.
evidence_refs: fact:In the 2000s, low inflation allowed labor market gains to translate into equity gains without tightening fears.
failure_condition: If, when CPIAUCSL YoY <2.5%, the correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward 20-day returns is not significantly positive (Rank IC<0.03) over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at the zero lower bound signals that the Fed is committed to an accommodative monetary policy stance, reducing short-term uncertainty and encouraging risk-taking, which leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:During the 2008-2012 zero lower bound period, equities rallied after each FOMC commitment to low rates.
failure_condition: If during any consecutive 2-year interval when FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, the 5-day forward return is negative more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) rises above its 2017 base level of 100, it indicates that manufacturing activity has recovered to pre-recession strength, signaling a self-sustaining expansion that boosts corporate earnings and equity prices over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO crossing 100 in 2010-2011 preceded positive equity returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO above 100 fails to produce a positive average 20-day forward return (mean > 0) with statistical significance at the 10% level over any 4-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: An improving labor market (rising PAYEMS) combined with a near-zero federal funds rate indicates demand growth without imminent monetary tightening, as the Fed is likely to remain accommodative until employment improves further, which bolsters equity valuations over a quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed's stated reaction function in 2012 linked rate increases to sustained employment improvement.
regime_hint: early recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields S&P 500 60-day forward returns that are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.05) over a 5-year period, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a liquidity trap (FEDFUNDS near zero and UNRATE above 8%), an increase in real personal spending (RSAFS) is more likely to be driven by fiscal stimulus or pent-up demand rather than credit pullback, and signals a durable recovery that positively impacts equities over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Liquidity trap conditions in 2010-2012 made consumption growth a reliable growth indicator.
failure_condition: If, when FEDFUNDS <0.25% and UNRATE>8%, the S&P 500 20-day forward return is not positively correlated with RSAFS growth (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year window, reject.
